Why Writing Posts Can Earn Money — Google AdSense and Liker, Explained

Did you know writing blog posts can earn you money? And did you know Google AdSense isn't the only path? This post is the intro version of how it works — Google AdSense (click revenue vs. impression revenue), how AdBlock affects you, what it takes to build real income, plus Liker as a like-based crypto-tipping alternative.
JavaScript Core (66) - ES6 Arrow Functions: The Gotchas That Trip Everyone Up

We've already covered plenty of differences between arrow functions and traditional functions, but there are a few situations that come up all the time in real code. This chapter rounds up the most common arrow function gotchas you'll actually hit in practice — returning objects, short-circuit assignment, and prototype methods.
JavaScript Core (65) — ES6 Arrow Functions: How They Differ From Regular Functions

Last chapter we said arrow functions are a new piece of ES6 syntax. So this time let's talk about how arrow functions actually differ from regular functions — no arguments object, no own this, can't be rebound with call/apply/bind, and no prototype.
Learning Python from a JavaScript Angle (31): So What Do You Want to Learn Next?
Learn Python From a JavaScript Angle

I finally crossed the finish line of the Ironman contest. So for day 31, the last one, I'm just going to sit down and chat with you about how the whole thing went — what I learned about Python coming from JavaScript, and why I think the real takeaway is to keep learning no matter what tool you pick up.
Learning Python From JavaScript (30): Building a Web Scraper

We've picked up a fair amount of Python by now, and this is the last day of the Ironman contest — so let's put it all together and build a simple scraper for the iThome Ironman site. Using Requests plus BeautifulSoup, we'll grab the topic list, page through each topic's contestants, and save everything out to JSON.
How to Change Chrome DevTools' Language

Chrome DevTools has always defaulted to English, but starting around Chrome 94 it added language localization. This post is the quick how-to — opening DevTools settings, switching to your preferred language under Preferences, and reloading DevTools to apply.
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (29) - BeautifulSoup
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle

We're heading into the home stretch of the Ironman contest, so this post covers a really common package: BeautifulSoup. It's one of the go-to tools for web scraping in Python, used to parse the HTML you download so you can pull out exactly the bits you want. Let's learn how to use it.
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (28) - Closures

JavaScript has this thing called a closure. Does Python have it too? In this chapter we'll have a relaxed chat about how to write a closure in Python if you want one — including the one syntax trap that'll throw a SyntaxError if you try to copy JavaScript too literally.
HMAC Authentication in Postman With Pre-Request Scripts (TDX API Example)

HMAC authentication is common for public APIs that need request-level signing, but Postman doesn't ship with HMAC support out of the box. This post walks through implementing HMAC in Postman via a pre-request script — using Taiwan's TDX (transport data) API as the concrete example, with jsSHA loaded via CDN inside the script.
Learning Python From a JavaScript Angle (27): Pass by Value or Pass by Reference?
第 13 屆 iT邦幫忙鐵人賽

We're almost at the end of the iThome Ironman series, and I just realized I skipped something kind of important — pass by value vs pass by reference. So this post is a quick chat about how values and references work in Python, and how they line up with what you already know from JavaScript.
